Guest Experience Manager - Sofitel Krabi Phokeethra Golf & Spa Resort.

sample_comments.title: First stay at Sofitel
sample_comments.text:
I guess I was expecting a well run resort but there were a few disappointments that I want to alert others planning to stay here.

The pool is magnificently designed and lagoon like with children friendly floating slides etc., But how could anybody swim in a 28-30C water temperature? It' s just downright uncomfortable cuz you feel like you are being cooked in the pool.

Secondly, the Maya restaurant closed from 4-6pm with last order at3:30p. After 4pm the a/c is turned off which means any guest who stays at the resort will have to order food to eat by the pool between 3:30-6pm. What about those who don't want to eat outdoor? Room service is of course an option., but it is simply unacceptable for a resort of this caliber to not offer all day indoor dining facility. Especially for guests who eat a full breakfast and probably won't want to eat or snack until after 3pm.

The wifi is definitely not up to snuff, there is no data flow even if your device shows all three bars of the wifi symbol. It's deceiving so please keep this in mind.

The room cleaning service is another disappointment. Even after hanging the clean room sign at the door around 11am, nobody came to clean room till after 4pm. And we arrived back in the room at 4pm when we saw the cleaning staff open our room door to try to get inside to clean. We of course turned him away cuz we needed our privacy in the room. There was a 5 hour window for the staff to clean but apparently staff were too busy elsewhere cleaning or they simply didn't even see the clean room sign. The sign is clearly useless.

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.positives:
This has been our third visit to the Sofitel Krabi. We discovered this fantastic hotel through a search for the longest warm-water pools in the world. Simply for the pool inaugurated by the princess, it is worth returning there.
Additionally, we are lovers of hotels with architectural and colonial interior design. We have become the most loyal customers of the Indian restaurant, where we established a very friendly relationship with Chef Mohabir, Restaurant Manager Ms. Kuhn Sao, and the team members Ms. Pahn, Ankit, Janak, Jib, Jan, and Maj, who served us with unparalleled professionalism and kindness, delighting our palates at every dinner.
Feeling at ease is generated through the five senses:
 • Sight: The hotel offers much beauty to behold.
 • Smell: The plants and deep cleanliness delighted our sense of smell.
 • Taste: The chef Mohabir's specialties are exceptional.
 • Touch: The skeletal-muscular system is cared for by professionals in one of Thailand's best spas, where soothing music and skilled hands induce peace of spirit.
However, unfortunately, as I mentioned and criticized in my text from last year, it is precisely the sense of hearing that suffers the most---indeed, it is mortified in this hotel. This compels me to refrain from giving the hotel the maximum stars in my rating. I propose an analysis by management regarding this matter. Once we leave the spa, speakers placed in the lobby or garden at various times fill the air with jarring sounds dominated by bass components, either to celebrate or to accompany social activities for children by the pool. I consider the high volume harmful to children's eardrums and unbearable for mature guests like us who come to relax.
In summary, a formidable vacation hotel experience!
Best regards,
Kuhn Egon

sample_comments.title: Very luxurious resort hotel
sample_comments.text:
My family stayed in this hotel for three nights and four days. Unexpectedly we realised that this hotel is very luxurious and upscale, considering the cost we paid. Hotel was very quiet and peaceful. Staff services were also very good. One of the best things about this hotel is breakfast. In particular, bakery section was excellent! The swimming pool was gigantic and clean. If you want to stay in hotel all day, then this is a good choice for your family (one suggestion to the hotel: it might be better if you have beach balls in swimming pool area).

One minor thing is location. Beach nearby is not good for swimming so you have to go to Ao Nang beach by taxi or hotel shuttle, which are very costly. There are few restaurants or marts nearby. If you have maps or GPS, renting a car is much cheaper.
sample_comments.positives: Hotel facility, swimming pool, staff

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.positives: Nice pool and kids loved it. Breakfast was great. Restaurant choices decent. Staff are nice but do need more during peak hours.
sample_comments.negatives: We knew it was not exactly a beach resort but activity center can organize tours in land or at sea. Hotel has character and hence feels a bit tiered.

sample_comments.title: Nice hotel rooms with a wonderful swimming pool
sample_comments.text:
The hotel is classic and classy, a bit old school but really well maintained. The room was huge and really clean, there is absolutely nothing negative to say about it a part from the TV which was 10 years old, but honestly who cares about the TV in such a tropical paradise.
The hotel staff was really well trained. They attended our few requests really fast. Another thing to underline, they were human unlike certain other hotel chains. Not the kind of staff who are reciting their text by memory like robots, they were truly interacting with the customers by having genuine conversation. It was pleasant to experience.
In terms of food, we really enjoyed the breakfast which provides a wide choice of food. Pancakes amongst other delicacies were delicious. We found that the bar/restaurant at the pool was too pricy, we found that 1000bahts for 2 sandwiches is a bit too much in Thailand especially when the taste had nothing special.

For the negative side, we found the hotel really noisy, because it is facing a road where many trucks are passing by. There were also work inside the hotel on Saturday. Even on the swimming pool the filters were so noisy, so finally it was difficult to be relaxed at 100%.

We were also expecting a private beach and we were quite disappointed. The beach is definitely not a place where you can lay down and tan. For the one who never went to Krabi before, don't go there for the beaches you will be disappointed especially compared to other places in Thailand. In addition to that be prepared to open your purse. From the hotel to Aonang will become costly on a daily basis (only few restaurants around).

So this place is only recommended for people (families with child I guess) who want to be in a secluded area with nothing much around. But for young couples in their 30's I guess there surely are better places to stay. So even if there were few positive aspects about this hotel, we will probably choose another one if we ever come back to Krabi.
sample_comments.positives: Spacious room, wonderful swimming pool, delicious breakfast

sample_comments.negatives: The beach is across the street and the kids club is really basic ! With not much activities

sample_comments.title: Great service and impressive pool
sample_comments.positives: The Sofitel's service and facilities are probably the best in Krabi. The staff are extremely professional and went well out of their way to ensure that we had a pleasant stay. The pool is definitely the property's most impressive facility. According to their marketing, it is the largest free-from pool in Thailand (or maybe just Krabi), but it is quite impressive, and the pool-side bar service was extremely attentive. The club lounge is also a pleasant facility and made our stay even more enjoyable.
sample_comments.negatives: The main thing to note about the Sofitel is that while it is technically "beachfront", getting to the beach requires walking across the lawn and crossing a road to what amounts to a not particularly impressive beach. This is why the pool is the main attraction at this property. It's also why you may not want to bother with an ocean view room. The modest traffic on the road wasn't noisy during our stay, but it also doesn't provide quite the picturesque view that you might expect.

sample_comments.title: Many activities, huge pool and nice for families
sample_comments.text:
We liked our stay in this resort because of it's very nice appearance and the many activities (windsurfing, sailing, golfing, thai boxing, bicycling, table tennis, tennis, running, kicker, gym, sauna, swimming, yoga, tai chi, ...) that were offered - mostly complimentary or at quite a reasonable price. The very huge pool is the biggest in Thailand one of the biggest in south east Asia. The beach made quite a mixed impression: on high tide, there won't be any beach at all, on very low tide the water begins some 50-100m behind the black rocks and on medium tide the water covers the rocks leaving a very nice, wide and sandy beach. The nearby island can be reached by the complimentary speed boat trip.
Unfortunately, WiFi (176 THB/30 minutes) as well as food and drinks are very expensive. The bed was much too hard, the A/C too strong and in an 5* hotel the TV should be a big LCD/LED not a small ray tube TV.
This resort is perfect for families, because of the many activities - also some of them special for kids.

The hotel should consider provide free shuttle van to the town (Ao Nang, only) instead of charging per way for a person. The hotel should considerate for guests travel in small group (less than 3).

We had a great stay here. Its a lovely but old hotel, hence not quite up to its 5 star rating at the moment.
The staff and service was great when we were there, the breakfast buffet was awesome - you can tell the French influence and the lady who gave the cooking class (Yala) was fantastic.
The kids club staff were great too.
Downsides were the big mine/ plant just down the road and the resulting stream of lorries going up and down, the shuttle into town was more expensive then local taxis - no brainer to fix that one... and the beach isn't great. Fun for crab hunting at night though.
Some past reviews suggested the food wasn't great but all our meals were really good and/ or its a only a short walk or taxi ride down the road to find lots of other cheaper local food places.

sample_comments.title: Great experience for family stay
sample_comments.text: The trip was very relaxing. With young children, my family choose to hang around the resort by the beach and pool-side majority of the time. Breakfast food is great with a wide variety of choices to pick from. Room is spacious and comfortable. Rooms are clean but can be slightly better. (strands of hair found on floor under the bed). Staff are all very courteous aside from one uncharacteristic rude encounter at the ice-cream shop (The staff may have been overworked and tired at the end of the day, giving her the benefit of the doubt). The children's play area had plenty of activities for kids. There is also a friendly staff to watch over the children. Overall, justifiable as a 5-star resort given the overall experience. Would recommend this place to families with young children.
sample_comments.positives: Close proximity to the beach (private); Large swimming pool and children's pool; Children's play area along with the gym and the food was great!

sample_comments.title: Hotel have very poor kitchen, breakfast very bad
sample_comments.text: Hotel 4* -
sample_comments.positives: Have nice tines curt and helpful staff

sample_comments.title: A good getaway
sample_comments.text:
We had a pleasant stay at the resort. The pool is said to be the largest in SEA, and indeed it was spacious enough to accommodate many folks without being too crowded. 

The area is away from shops so take note that if there is a downpour in the afternoon you might be left stranded in the hotel with not much food options as the restaurants open only for dinner at 6pm. Only a cafe sells a tiny selection of simple sandwiches. So do keep some snacks stocked.

Staff was very attentive and helpful. A bit like Club Med, the resort provides sufficient activities to keep you occupied. Nice to catch the sunset at the resort's beach. Overall a good stay and would return.
sample_comments.positives: Amenities, staff and pool

sample_comments.title: Big pool, golf course average condition

They both went out of the way, to take care of us, when my girlfriend fell sick. Kudos to them and the rest of the staff.

sample_comments.positives: The standard, the Buffet breakfast, the pool

sample_comments.title: relaxing for adult enjoyable for kids
sample_comments.text:
We had a very pleasant stay. It was an all girls holiday. 2 mums and 3 little girls. Check in was very smooth. The kids were also welcomed, greeted and had their own "check in"by the staff. You could see that the girls felt really special. When the rooms were ready, staff personally showed us to the room. Explained and pointed us is the general direction of the facilities in the hotel. And when it came to showing us where the kids club was, they specifically spoke to the kids and gave them the instuctions on how to get there. As a mother, I felt very comfortable leaving the girls at the kids club. The staff there are really child friendly people. Although I personally think it could do with a little refurbishment on the inside.
Breakfast had a good spread but we found that lunch was a problem. Menu at the poolside was limited. Other restaurants closed except the coffee house which only served sandwiches drinks and desserts. No biggy there!
We had a few hiccups at the hotel. There was a problem with the pool pump. So water was murky and sandy. On the second last day we didn't have water in the rooms. But the problem was rectified quickly. On the whole, I had a very relaxing stay. The kids loved it there. We never felt the need to venture out of the hotel. The activities desk arranged an elephant ride and a visit to an island. Which all turned out perfectly. The selling point of this hotel apart from the pool and lovely grounds it sits on is the staff. They go the extra mile to make your day just right. My heartfelt thanks to all staff.....reception, activities desk, kids club, dining, housekeeping and just everyone. The kids are looking froward to seeing you again.
sample_comments.positives: attentive staff, great selection for breakfast and the atmosphere in the hotel was perfect for for all ages

sample_comments.title: Better Staff training could make it excellent
sample_comments.text:
Very nice property..one of the few that I would rate as ideal for guests from a cross-section - honeymooners as well as families with kids. The big expansive pool is nicely landscaped with hammocks hanging invitingly across the lawns surrounding the pool. Breakfast lounge menu gives you nothing to complain about. They have a nice coffee shop , besides an Indian/Thai restaurant and of course the French restaurant and a bar. There's also Reading room, Kid's centre , Gym, Tennis court and a small golf course.

Rooms are spacious and well appointed - ask for 1st or 2nd floor rooms if you need sea views.
Krabi has lots of outdoor options and the activity centre offers in house booking..of course you pay a premium against street prices for the same tours/activities.

What could be better ? - we had a couple of frustrations with the house keeping service. as well as front desk....all staff are extremely polite and always smiling and cheerful ..the issue is around their ability to clearly understand requests and instructions in English (understandably though)..sometimes even basic questions or requests are met with a smiling but completely blank expression and you know you are in for a prolonged attempt to articulate what you need...we actually gave up on a couple of occasions :)-
sample_comments.positives: Top class property , Breakfast menu,location , Room size

sample_comments.title: 1 night in Krabi Sofitel
sample_comments.positives: We like the facilities and the service as well as all the options available for visiting the island.
sample_comments.negatives: The restaurants are very expensive and not the best..

sample_comments.title: Nice hotel with great facilities
sample_comments.text: We enjoyed the pool very much. The pool bar/restaurant served good drinks and food. The staff was very attentive and pleasant. Many activities were thoughtfully programmed for people's interests and were properly organized.
sample_comments.positives: The large swimming pool

sample_comments.title: The Sofitel was lovely and met our high expectations.
sample_comments.positives: The breakfast is a buffet with a large range of options. The pool is the largest in Thailand. Room was large and comfy. Restaurants were good - Italian and Asian. Nice spa. Beach access across the street. Good excursions - we went to Hong Island.
sample_comments.negatives: This isn't a negative, just an observation. As a couple who enjoyed the privacy and ambiance at Rayavadee, the Sofitel is a larger resort hotel that caters more to families and golfers.

sample_comments.title: Beyond expectation
sample_comments.text:
we had a pleasant stay at sofitel and it was definitely beyond our expectation. the hotel is huge and beautiful and the staff are really friendly and helpful, the kind that would go the extra mile to make your stay a pleasant one. 

the swimming pool is big and long! not to forget, it is slightly heated..how awesome is that? we spent most of our time at the pool, swimming and sometimes just sitting at the pool bench, waiting for sunset. the view is fantastic as it right opposite the beach.

the hotel offers a lot of packages at very reasonable prices. for instance, we took the hong island package which costs us around per person and we got a return transfer by sofitel's luxury speedboat, beach towel, beach mat, a butler who will be there to help you in case you need any assistance, lunch and drinks, and also bread for the fishes! definitely worth every penny spent.

if you are on your honeymoon or anniversary vacation, be sure to tell the staff. we got a complimentary happy honeymoon cake during our stay :) 

the only downside is there is no free wi-fi or internet access. you have to pay thb100per hour or thb235 per day to access the internet. i think this is a bit inconvenient as i feel handicapped without internet!!
sample_comments.positives: Friendly and helpful staff, awesome swimming pool

sample_comments.title: Excellent Stay, highlly recoomended for families
sample_comments.positives:
They have a huge pool with areas dedicated for children and adults as well. The pool bar is also nice and serves finger food as well. The facilities are fantastic with golf course inside the report only. 
If i go again to Krabi, ill stay again.
sample_comments.negatives: It a bit away from the main city thus no hustle and bustle; but i didnt mind as massage parlors, grocery and few restaurants are close by. Breakfast can improve in terms of food taste.

sample_comments.title: fabulous resort
sample_comments.text:
Sofitel was fantastic.

Krabi's beaches are some of the best to be found anywhere and lie within easy access from the main town of Krabi. 

Each has its own attractions to offer the tourist -- be it simple relaxation in the sun and sea, seclusion, or the opportunity to indulge in water sports and other activities in the ultimate tropical paradise.

definitely recommend it

The doors are designed to slam on there own or if you try to close slow they still slam, it's possible to close quietly but that involves your key card which nobody does, long story short, you hear a lot of slamming doors that night before bed and in the morning as people are going to breakfast.

sample_comments.title: nice classy hotel, good friendly service
sample_comments.text:
nice big rooms, very comfortable to say the least. there's a nice big pool if you care to go for a dip or a swim. 

the location is quite far from town, works well if you just like to stay in and enjoy the place. on the other hand, there's free shuttle to aonang beach town.

gym is small though.

sample_comments.title: Amazing for a weekend getaway!
sample_comments.positives: The hotel is very luxurious. It's so spacious and the pool is one of a kind. The staff are so kind and friendly, and the bar in the evening with live music is great fun. The location is great for a quiet and relaxing getaway, with the beach quite literally a few steps away. The breakfast was amazing! Such a variety! There are lots of places to eat if you walk down the road, so you're not subjected to only eating at the hotel for breakfast, lunch and dinner.
sample_comments.negatives: The hotel is a little outdated, it could do with some paint in areas. The pool water was almost too hot (but this is not a fault of the hotel), we just happened to be there during a heatwave, but might want to bear it in mind.

sample_comments.title: Excellent hotel
sample_comments.text: The hotel has a huge swimming pool in a beautiful garden. However since it is quite new, there is little shade as the trees are still young. The beach is unsuitable for swimming. There are hardly any chairs and no umbrellas on the beach. Whereas the beach itself is sandy, once entering there are plenty of (sharp) stones in the water. The location is somehow far from where action takes place, so you are more or less dependent on the facilities of the hotel.
sample_comments.positives: Rooms, breakfast is excellent

sample_comments.title: Excellent
sample_comments.positives: The pool is very nice. Breakfast was great as well.

sample_comments.title: Excellent
sample_comments.positives: The best swimming pool I have seen with loads of activities. Very good resort

sample_comments.title: Super GRAND!!!
sample_comments.text: I was very impressed with the immense hotel grounds and long drive way to get to the open hotel lobby, which had huge doorways, high ceilings and was one of the few places in Thailand, never mind South East Asia that gave that Grand, Executive feeling that will make you feel underdressed in flip flops, shorts and a T-shirt. Because of the sheer size of the hotel, with hundreds of rooms, the walk to our room seemed to take a while but it was well worth it when we got to our room which had a good view of the pool and the ocean. The room was very large, with a very traditional, colonial feel to it. It didn't take us long to get into our swimming wear and head down to the pool which was by far the biggest I've ever seen in my life at a whopping 16,000 sq m!!! It seemed to go on for ever. What I particularly liked was the food we ordered from the bar. Yes the prices were Western standard but well worth it. The Roast Beef sandwich and tropical shake went down very well sitting on the stools, half submerged in the pool. After a very relaxing day in the pool, we just crossed the road, walked along the beach and drove into tow in our rented car later in the evening for some dinner. This is the perfect place to stay on the last day of your trip in Krabi if you're tired of activities and just want to spend your time relaxing by the pool or on the beach before catching the taxi to the airport.
sample_comments.positives:
- Ridiculously large pool! 
- Great pool side bar menu!
- Beautiful large grounds
- quiet location
- A hotel that can boast 5 stars for its executive feel.
- Large, comfortable rooms.
- Close to beach
sample_comments.negatives:
- Prices are a bit steep for Thailand. Breakfast buffet in particular!
- Located in Klong Muang, so it is a bit far from town, hence pricey taxi rides. Good if you prefer to be away from Ao Nang and in isolation.
- A road in between the beach and hotel grounds with trucks driving by now and then.

The delay in serving food in one of the dinner was compensated with a genuine apology and good care of us on our next visit.

But if want to go to ao nang, krabi town night market and airport is a bit far. In conclusion i still like the Sofitel Luxury Hotel quite much.

sample_comments.text: The transfer was 45 minutes late to pick us up from the airport. It took several phone calls to find out how long until we were to be picked up. Reception staff were very apologetic and looked after us when we arrived. Tried to order some lunch at the poolside but the stsff didnt want to walk over to where i was sitting to bring it to me. The Christmas dinner and entertainment was excellent. Overall the stay was good.

sample_comments.title: Very good and worhty hotel for vacation
sample_comments.text: Though the distance to Aonang beach is pretty far (20 mins by hotel shuttle), but it's worthy to stay in such nice hotel, big swimming pool, and lot of activities arranged by hotel, which made your vacation really relaxed and enjoyable. Will come back if have plan to visit Krabi again.
sample_comments.positives: Very nice hotel stuff, good service, and nice hotel.

sample_comments.title: Beautiful Hotel
sample_comments.text: We had an enjoyable 3 day break at the Sofitel resort. On arrival, we were immediately up graded. The room was spacious and over looked the small golf course. The best feature was the pool, the largest in Thailand. Food was expensive compared to other hotels but there are a number of small restaurants within walking distance. A great place to relax at the end of a busy holiday.
sample_comments.positives: Huge Swimming Pool

sample_comments.title: Beautiful hotel with great hospitality and smiles
sample_comments.text: The hotel is located in a quiet location away from Ao Nang which is touristic. The hotel shutter to and from Ao Nang makes it convenient if you want to go out for lunch and dinner with more varieties and economical prices (although the shutter costs per person per way). All hotel facilities are clean and oriental. Big pool and plenty of space for sunbathing. The best rated for our stay is the hospitality and smiles from the hotel staff, making you feel too important sometimes :)
sample_comments.positives: Very friendly and attentive staff, cozy ambient, big swimming pool and happy hour drinks
sample_comments.negatives: Food in the hotel is quite expensive (for Thais at least), no in-room music

sample_comments.title: Sofitel Paradise
sample_comments.text: The hotel itself is lovely. It's set in beautiful surroundings. The hotel lobby is open plan and it looks on to the sea which is lovely. The rooms are spacious and clean, and the staff are very attentive. The swimming pool is amazing. Amazing choice for breakfast it's not the best I've ever had but it won't disappoint. If you are looking for tranquility this is the place to stay. There really isn't much to dislike about the Hotel. The only thing I would say is Sofitel charge you for transfer to Au nang. Personally I think this service should be complimentary considering the price you pay for the hotel. The only other thing I would say is that Klong Muang beach is not very nice! but I do think the hotel makes up for it by having the largest swimming pool in Thailand and also offers a free transfer to Hong Island which has a beautiful beach and perfect setting for sunbathing and snorkeling.
sample_comments.positives: Set in beautiful surroundings. Staff very attentive and respectful. Largest Swimming pool in Thailand. Hotel offers complimentary transfer to Hong Island. Good value for money
sample_comments.negatives:
Lack of local shops/restaurants near hotel.
Klong Muang beach is horrible.
Charges for transfers to Au Nang.

sample_comments.title: Nice place - good service
sample_comments.text: The hotel can be found in a quite and secluded place of Krabi province, however, it also means that there aren't many attractions nearby the hotel. Fortunately two really good restaurants are only minutes away - the Krabi Sand and Joo's kitchen. Though the Sofitel's beach isn't really attractive, you won't be bothered by that since the pool is excellent. Furthermore, Sofitel operates a daily free shuttle boat service to Hong Island, which has a really nice beach to swim, snorkel or simply lie down.
sample_comments.positives: Great pool and superb shuttle boat service to Hong Island.
sample_comments.negatives: The restaurants of the hotel are not only expensive (comparing to other restaurants in the neighborhood), but also not the best in quality.

secondary.description_full:
Sofitel Krabi Phokeethra Golf and Spa Resort - Nơi tận hưởng kỳ nghỉ tuyệt vời

Sofitel Krabi Phokeethra Golf and Spa Resort là một khách sạn 5.0 sao tọa lạc tại thành phố Krabi, Thái Lan. Với vị trí thuận lợi, khách sạn này gần trung tâm thành phố chỉ cách 20 km và cách sân bay 40 phút đi xe. Khách sạn Sofitel Krabi Phokeethra Golf and Spa Resort không chỉ là một nơi nghỉ dưỡng sang trọng mà còn mang đến cho du khách những trải nghiệm tuyệt vời với đội ngũ nhân viên chuyên nghiệp và tận tâm. Đánh giá về hiệu suất của nhân viên đạt điểm số 8.8/10, cho thấy sự chất lượng và sự phục vụ tốt nhất từ đội ngũ nhân viên tại đây. 

Khách sạn Sofitel Krabi Phokeethra Golf and Spa Resort cũng được đánh giá cao về sạch sẽ với điểm số ấn tượng là 8.9/10. Du khách có thể yên tâm tận hưởng kỳ nghỉ mà không phải lo lắng về vấn đề vệ sinh. Dù vị trí của khách sạn được đánh giá tương đối ở mức 7.3/10, nhưng nhờ vào các tiện ích và dịch vụ tuyệt vời, khách sạn này vẫn là sự lựa chọn hàng đầu của du khách khi đến Krabi.

Sofitel Krabi Phokeethra Golf and Spa Resort - khách sạn 5.0 sao tại Krabi.

Ngoài tiêu chuẩn SHA Plus, tất cả khách đều được truy cập Wi-Fi miễn phí trong tất cả các phòng và đỗ xe miễn phí nếu đến bằng ô tô. Nằm ở vị trí trung tâm tại Klong Muang của Krabi, chỗ nghỉ này đặt quý khách ở gần các điểm thu hút và tùy chọn ăn uống thú vị. Đừng rời đi trước khi ghé thăm Bãi biển Ao Nang nổi tiếng. Được xếp hạng 5 sao, chỗ nghỉ chất lượng cao này cho phép khách nghỉ sử dụng mát-xa, bể bơi ngoài trời và bồn tắm nước nóng ngay trong khuôn viên.
Tiện ích: Ban công/sân hiên, Bi-a, Bàn tiếp tân [24 giờ], Bãi đỗ xe [miễn phí], Bãi đỗ xe [tại chỗ], Bãi đỗ xe có nhân viên, Bình chữa cháy, Bóng bàn, Băng niêm phong phòng dành cho khách sau khi vệ sinh, Bơi với ống thở, Bơi xuồng, Bảo vệ [24 giờ], Bếp, Bể bơi, Bể bơi [trẻ em], Bể bơi [ngoài trời], Bệnh xá, Bồn tắm, Bồn tắm nước nóng, Bữa sáng, Bữa sáng [tự chọn], Bữa sáng kiểu Á, Bữa sáng trong phòng, Bữa ăn cho trẻ, CCTV bên ngoài chỗ nghỉ, CCTV trong khu vực chung, CLB trẻ em, Chuyến du lịch, Chuông/liên lạc nội bộ không dây, Các môn thể thao dưới nước (không có động cơ).
Điểm đánh giá 8.5/10 dựa trên 1944 đánh giá.
Khách hay nhắc đến: Dịch vụ, Bể bơi, Bữa sáng, Địa điểm, Gia đình, Cơ sở vật chất cho trẻ em, Độ sạch sẽ, Nhiều lựa chọn nhà hàng.
